---
title: "Uninstalled ExpressVpn and can't connect to the internet"
date: 2017-01-01
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by crander2 on 2017-01-01
Hi All,

I've uninstalled ExpressVpn and now I can not connect to the internet.

When I run this command sudo vi /etc/resolv.conf I get the below result:

 # Generated by expressvpn
search expressvpn
nameserver 10.19.0.1
~                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
"/etc/resolv.conf" [readonly] 3L, 65C   

I think the fix is restoring the resolv.conf back to its original state (this is just a guess).

I'm currently running Ubuntu 16.10 (Yakkety Yak).

Any assistance in solving this issue would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks

crander2

---

### Post by jeremy31 on 2017-01-01
This might reset it to normal ```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure resolvconf
```
Reboot

---

### Post by crander2 on 2017-01-01
Hi Jeremy,

Your suggested fix worked - thanks for that.

You just have to log in as root user to run that command.

Cheers

crander2
